name: Paracetamol
use: Mild to moderate pain relief and reducing fever.
dosage: Adults: 500mg-1000mg every 4-6 hours. Maximum 4000mg per 24 hours.
warning: Do not exceed the maximum dose. Can cause liver damage if taken in excess. Avoid alcohol.

name: Ibuprofen
dosage: Adults: 200mg-400mg every 4-6 hours as needed. Maximum 1200mg per 24 hours without prescription.
warning: Take with food to avoid stomach upset. Do not use if you have a history of stomach ulcers or severe heart failure.

name: Oral Rehydration Salts (ORS)
use: Treating dehydration caused by diarrhea or vomiting.
dosage: Mix one sachet in 1 liter of clean, boiled and cooled water. Drink frequently.
warning: Use only safe, clean water to mix. Seek medical help if diarrhea persists for more than 2 days or if there is blood in stool.
